hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding with a GRU-based sequence encoder linked through a multi-head cross-attention mechanism is trained on high-throughput MD-derived binding free energies, then it will more accurately predict polymer-surface adhesion energies than baseline models using simpler or single-modality encoders, because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the interaction-dependent complementarity between polymer sequence features and surface spatial patterns that governs binding affinity.
